message:
feat(db): add storage_root-aware pool keying, project identity reconciliation, and archive drift detection

Introduce storage_root as an explicit pool configuration parameter to prevent
cache aliasing when the same SQLite file is opened with different archive roots.
Previously, pool caching used only the database path as identity, which could
alias unrelated mailboxes when the archive storage root differed.

Key changes:

- DbPoolConfig gains storage_root: Option<PathBuf> field with resolved_storage_root()
  accessor; pool_cache_key and sqlite_init_gate incorporate storage_root into
  their identity keys
- Pool acquire passes storage_root through to the init gate and init_sqlite_db
  so archive reconciliation uses the correct root
- MailboxProjectIdentity struct models canonical project identity (slug +
  human_key) with fuzzy matching via normalized tokens and basename extraction
- collect_db_project_identities queries the projects table and returns a
  BTreeSet of identities for drift comparison
- archive_missing_project_identities compares archive project identities against
  DB identities to find projects present in the archive but not yet in the
  SQLite index
- ArchiveMessageInventory extended with projects, agents, and
  project_identities fields for richer reconciliation
- reconcile_archive_state_before_init uses the expanded inventory to detect
  project-level drift, not just message-count drift
- query_database_inventory_for_reconcile renamed from
  query_database_message_inventory_for_reconcile and returns a structured
  ReconcileInventory including project/agent counts and identities

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
